message.content: Adversaries may exploit Regsvcs and Regasm, trusted Windows command-line utilities used for registering .NET COM assemblies, to execute malicious code. These utilities, often digitally signed by Microsoft, can bypass application controls by leveraging attributes like [ComRegisterFunction] or [ComUnregisterFunction] to run code during registration or unregistration processes. This technique allows code execution even with insufficient privileges. Commonly, malware such as Agent Tesla has utilized this method to drop malicious files onto systems. The potential impact includes unauthorized code execution, system compromise, and evasion of security controls. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and restricting the use of Regsvcs and Regasm, implementing application whitelisting, and employing behavioral analysis to detect abnormal usage patterns of these utilities.
